items.title: Match pretext to downstream modality and task
items.avoid: Using a text-only MLM checkpoint as the vision backbone for a medical imaging classifier without domain adaptation.
items.prefer: Choose pretext tasks native to the modality (MLM for text, contrastive or masked-patch for vision) and validate with linear probes on in-domain labels.
items.why: Representations encode whatever the surrogate task rewards; modality mismatch wastes compute and hurts transfer.
items.example: Pretrain a ViT with masked patch prediction on radiology images, then fine-tune on 500 labeled scans — not BERT embeddings on pixels.

items.title: Evaluate representations, not only pretext loss
items.avoid: Stopping when contrastive loss plateaus without checking downstream accuracy on a labeled validation set.
items.prefer: Run periodic linear probes or small fine-tuning runs on a fixed labeled subset to track representation quality during pretrain.
items.why: Pretext loss can decrease while representations overfit shortcuts useless for the target task.
items.example: Every 10k steps, train a frozen-encoder logistic regression head on 1k labeled CIFAR-10 images and log accuracy.

items.title: Separate pretrain and fine-tune data responsibly
items.avoid: Leaking downstream test labels into pretext construction or tuning hyperparameters on the test set.
items.prefer: Keep pretraining on unlabeled train split only; use a held-out labeled validation set for model selection before final test evaluation.
items.why: SSL scale makes overfitting to evaluation benchmarks easy if splits are not strictly isolated.
items.example: Pretrain on unlabeled Common Crawl shards; fine-tune on supervised SST-2 train only; report once on SST-2 test.
